title: Features Object
description: The features collection which provides access to all existing features. This collection provides direct access to all features regardless of type. It also provides access to type specific collections where you can get features of a specific type and also create new features of that type.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createPath
methods.description: Method that creates a Path used to define the shape of a Sweep feature. A Path is a contiguous set of curves that can be a combination of sketch curves and model edges.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: arrangeFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the Arrange features within the component and supports the creation of new Arrange features.

properties.name: baseFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the existing base features and supports the creation of new base features. A base feature represents a body that is non-parametric.

properties.name: bossFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the boss features within the component and supports the creation of new boss features.

properties.name: boundaryFillFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the Boundary Fill features within the component and supports the creation of new Boundary Fill features.

properties.name: boxFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the existing box features.

properties.name: chamferFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the chamfer features within the component and supports the creation of new chamfer features.

properties.name: circularPatternFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the circular pattern features within the component and supports the creation of new circular pattern features.

properties.name: coilFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the Coil Primitive features within the component.

properties.name: combineFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the combine features within the component and supports the creation of new combine features.

properties.name: copyPasteBodies
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the existing copy-paste features and supports the creation of new copy-paste features.

properties.name: cornerClosureFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the existing Corner Closure features.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of features in the collection.

properties.name: customFeatures

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: loftFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to the existing loft features and supports the creation of new loft features.

properties.name: mergeFacesFeatures
properties.description: Returns the collection object that supports the ability to merge faces. Merging faces is currently limited to a Direct Modeling design or a body in a base feature. The result of merging faces is a direct B-Rep modification, and the change is not represented as a feature in the browser.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: draftFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the draftFeatures.add method. To use this sample, have a design open that contains at least one body. When you run the sample, you will be prompted to select the face to draft. Because the pull direction is using the base X-Y plane, you need to select a face that is not parallel to the X-Y plane.

related_samples.name: extendFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extendFeatures.add method. To use this sample, have a design open that contains at least one surface body. When you run the sample, you will be prompted to select an open edge of the body.

related_samples.name: Extend Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extend feature.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.addSimple
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.addSimple method.

related_samples.name: Extrude Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using setOneSideExtent
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the setOneSideExtent method. To use this sample have a design open that contains a sketch with a profile. When you run the script you will be prompted to select the profile that will be used to create the extrusion.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using setSymmetricExtent
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the setSymmetricExtent method. To use this sample have a design open that contains a sketch with a profile. When you run the script you will be prompted to select the profile that will be used to create the extrusion.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using thin extrude
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the setThinExtrude method. To use this sample have a design open that contains a sketch with a profile. When you run the script you will be prompted to select the profile that will be used to create the thin extrusion.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using ThroughAllExtent
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the ThroughAllExtent method.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using setTwoSidesExtent
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the setTwoSidesExtent method. To use this sample have a design open that contains a profile and a body that is positioned away from the profile but is a size where when the profile is extruded it will intersect the body. When you run the script you will be prompted to select the profile that will be used to create the extrusion and the body to extrude to. The extrusion will be created up to the body with an offset and will also be offset from the sketch plane.

related_samples.name: Move Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new move feature.

related_samples.name: Manage Participant Bodies API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to manage participant During Cut operation. Same API can be used to manage participants During intersection also.

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

related_samples.name: ReplaceFace Feature
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new replaceface feature.

related_samples.name: Shell Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new shell feature.

related_samples.name: Simple Extrusion Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new extrusion feature, resulting in a new component.

classType.title: Features.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.Features.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::Features::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createPath.title: Features.createPath Method
createPath.description: Method that creates a Path used to define the shape of a Sweep feature. A Path is a contiguous set of curves that can be a combination of sketch curves and model edges.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = features_var.createPath(curve)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = features_var.createPath(curve, isChain)

parameters.name: curve
parameters.description: A SketchCurve or an ObjectCollection containing multiple sketch entities and/or BRepEdge objects. If a single sketch curve or edge is input the isChain argument is checked to determine if connected curves (they do not need to be tangent) should be automatically found. If multiple curves are provided the isChain argument is always treated as false so you must provide all of the curves in the object collection that you want included in the path. The provided curves must all connect together in a single path.   The input curves can be from multiple sketches and bodies and they need to geometrically connect for a valid path to be created.

parameters.name: isChain
parameters.description: Optional argument, that defaults to true. If this argument is set to true, all curves and edges that are end point connected to the single input curve will be found and used to create the path. This argument is only used when the first argument is a single SketchCurve/BRepEdge object.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

related_samples.name: Sweep with guide rail Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Sweep feature that uses a guide rail along with a profile.

item.title: Features.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified feature using an index into the collection.

syntax.python: returnValue = features_var.item(index)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = features_var->item(index);

item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: Features.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified feature using the name of the feature.

syntax.python: returnValue = features_var.itemByName(name)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = features_var->itemByName(name);

itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if a feature matching the name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the same name seen in the timeline.
